questions.question: The three rectangles show the arrangements of the particles in each of the three states of matter. X, Y and Z represent the processes needed to change from one state to another. X Y Z What are the processes X, Y and Z? X                   Y                   Z A melting                 condensing                 evaporating, B evaporating                melting                   freezing, C melting                    freezing                    condensing, D freezing                evaporating                condensing

questions.question: Which equations are balanced? 1     Fe 2 O 3 +  3CO  →  2Fe  +  3CO 2 2     ZnCO 3 +  2HCl  →  ZnCl 2 +  CO 2 +  2H 2 O 3     Mg(NO 3 ) 2 +  NaOH  →  Mg(OH) 2 +  2NaNO 3 4     CaCO 3 +  H 2 SO 4 →  CaSO 4 +  H 2 O  +  CO 2 A 1 and 2 B    1 and 4 C    2 and 3 D    3 and 4
questions.text: Which equations are balanced? 1     Fe 2 O 3 +  3CO  →  2Fe  +  3CO 2 2     ZnCO 3 +  2HCl  →  ZnCl 2 +  CO 2 +  2H 2 O 3     Mg(NO 3 ) 2 +  NaOH  →  Mg(OH) 2 +  2NaNO 3 4     CaCO 3 +  H 2 SO 4 →  CaSO 4 +  H 2 O  +  CO 2 A 1 and 2 B    1 and 4 C    2 and 3 D    3 and 4
options: A 1 and 2 B    1 and 4 C    2 and 3 D    3 and 4

questions.question: The equation for the combustion of methane is shown. CH 4 +  2O 2 →  CO 2 +  2H 2 O Which mass of methane produces 36 g of water? A 16 g B    18 g C    32 g D    64 g 4
questions.text: The equation for the combustion of methane is shown. CH 4 +  2O 2 →  CO 2 +  2H 2 O Which mass of methane produces 36 g of water? A 16 g B    18 g C    32 g D    64 g 4
options: A 16 g B    18 g C    32 g D    64 g 4

questions.question: What  is  produced  at  each  electrode  during  the  electrolysis  of  aqueous  solutions  using  inert electrodes? positive electrode (anode) negative electrode (cathode) A metals or hydrogen non-metals only, B metals or oxygen non-metals only, C non-metals only metals or hydrogen, D non-metals only metals or oxygen
questions.text: What  is  produced  at  each  electrode  during  the  electrolysis  of  aqueous  solutions  using  inert electrodes? positive electrode (anode) negative electrode (cathode) A metals or hydrogen non-metals only, B metals or oxygen non-metals only, C non-metals only metals or hydrogen, D non-metals only metals or oxygen
options: A metals or hydrogen non-metals only
B metals or oxygen non-metals only
C non-metals only metals or hydrogen
D non-metals only metals or oxygen

questions.question: Which statement about a hydrogen-oxygen fuel cell in a car is correct? A The fuel cell produces heat, which powers the car., B The fuel cell is supplied with hydrogen directly from the air., C The only emission from the fuel cell is nitrogen gas, which is non-polluting., D The fuel cell produces electricity, which powers an electric motor.
questions.text: Which statement about a hydrogen-oxygen fuel cell in a car is correct? A The fuel cell produces heat, which powers the car., B The fuel cell is supplied with hydrogen directly from the air., C The only emission from the fuel cell is nitrogen gas, which is non-polluting., D The fuel cell produces electricity, which powers an electric motor.
options: A The fuel cell produces heat, which powers the car.
B The fuel cell is supplied with hydrogen directly from the air.
C The only emission from the fuel cell is nitrogen gas, which is non-polluting.
D The fuel cell produces electricity, which powers an electric motor.

questions.question: The reaction pathway diagram for a reaction is shown. energy progress of reaction reactants products Which statements are correct? 1     The reaction is exothermic. 2     The reaction is endothermic. 3     The temperature of the surroundings increases. 4     The temperature of the surroundings decreases. A 1 and 3 B    1 and 4 C    2 and 3 D    2 and 4
questions.text: The reaction pathway diagram for a reaction is shown. energy progress of reaction reactants products Which statements are correct? 1     The reaction is exothermic. 2     The reaction is endothermic. 3     The temperature of the surroundings increases. 4     The temperature of the surroundings decreases. A 1 and 3 B    1 and 4 C    2 and 3 D    2 and 4
options: A 1 and 3 B    1 and 4 C    2 and 3 D    2 and 4

questions.text: Which process involves a chemical change? A adding sodium to water, B boiling    water, C dissolving sodium chloride in water, D producing water from aqueous sodium chloride 5

D producing water from aqueous sodium chloride 5

questions.question: Which solids react with dilute sulfuric acid to form aqueous magnesium sulfate? 1     magnesium 2     magnesium     hydroxide 3     magnesium     nitrate 4     magnesium     oxide A 1, 2 and 4 B    1 and 3 C    2, 3 and 4 D    2 and 4 only
questions.text: Which solids react with dilute sulfuric acid to form aqueous magnesium sulfate? 1     magnesium 2     magnesium     hydroxide 3     magnesium     nitrate 4     magnesium     oxide A 1, 2 and 4 B    1 and 3 C    2, 3 and 4 D    2 and 4 only
options: A 1, 2 and 4 B    1 and 3 C    2, 3 and 4 D    2 and 4 only

questions.question: Which statements about an aqueous acid are correct? 1     Ammonia is formed when solid ammonium nitrate is added to an aqueous acid. 2     Effervescence is seen when sodium carbonate is added to an aqueous acid. 3     Methyl orange becomes yellow when added to an aqueous acid. 4     Red litmus remains red when added to an aqueous acid. A 1 and 3 B    1 and 4 C    2 and 3 D    2 and 4
questions.text: Which statements about an aqueous acid are correct? 1     Ammonia is formed when solid ammonium nitrate is added to an aqueous acid. 2     Effervescence is seen when sodium carbonate is added to an aqueous acid. 3     Methyl orange becomes yellow when added to an aqueous acid. 4     Red litmus remains red when added to an aqueous acid. A 1 and 3 B    1 and 4 C    2 and 3 D    2 and 4
options: A 1 and 3 B    1 and 4 C    2 and 3 D    2 and 4

questions.question: Chlorine, bromine and iodine are in the same group of the Periodic Table. Which statements about these three elements are correct? 1     Iodine is more reactive than chlorine. 2     They are diatomic covalent molecules. 3     They are all gases at room temperature. 4     Their atoms have seven electrons in their outer shell. A 1 and 3 B    1 and 4 C    2 and 3 D    2 and 4

A 1 and 3 B    1 and 4 C    2 and 3 D    2 and 4
options: A 1 and 3 B    1 and 4 C    2 and 3 D    2 and 4

questions.question: Iron nails are stored in an airtight container. lid air iron nails The nails begin to rust after a few days. How can the rusting of the nails be prevented? A Leave the lid off., B Replace the air with argon., C Put the container in a warm place., D Seal the container in a bag. 9
questions.text: Iron nails are stored in an airtight container. lid air iron nails The nails begin to rust after a few days. How can the rusting of the nails be prevented? A Leave the lid off., B Replace the air with argon., C Put the container in a warm place., D Seal the container in a bag. 9
options: B Replace the air with argon.
C Put the container in a warm place.
D Seal the container in a bag. 9

questions.text: Which test is used to show that a sample of water is pure? A Evaporate the water to see if any solids remain., B Heat the water to check its boiling point., C Test with anhydrous cobalt( II) chloride., D Use universal indicator paper to check its pH.
options: A Evaporate the water to see if any solids remain.
B Heat the water to check its boiling point.
C Test with anhydrous cobalt( II) chloride.
D Use universal indicator paper to check its pH.
